rules.label: Prohibition of subliminal or manipulative/deceptive AI techniques
rules.obligation: An AI system that deploys subliminal, purposefully manipulative, or deceptive techniques that materially distort behaviour and are reasonably likely to cause significant harm shall not be placed on the market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: All operators placing on market, putting into service, or using AI systems

rules.label: Prohibition of exploitation of vulnerabilities
rules.obligation: An AI system that exploits vulnerabilities due to age, disability, or a specific social or economic situation to materially distort behaviour in a manner likely to cause significant harm shall not be placed on the market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: All operators placing on market, putting into service, or using AI systems

rules.label: Prohibition of social scoring
rules.obligation: AI systems for evaluation or classification of persons based on social behaviour or personal characteristics leading to unjustified/disproportionate or out-of-context detrimental treatment shall not be placed on market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: All operators of AI systems performing social scoring

rules.label: Prohibition of individual criminal risk assessment based solely on profiling
rules.obligation: AI systems for assessing or predicting the risk of a person committing a criminal offence based solely on profiling or personality traits shall not be placed on market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: Operators of criminal risk-assessment AI systems
exceptions: Does not apply to AI systems supporting human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to a criminal activity

rules.label: Prohibition of untargeted facial image scraping
rules.obligation: AI systems that create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage shall not be placed on market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: Operators of facial recognition database AI systems

rules.label: Prohibition of emotion inference in workplace and education
rules.obligation: AI systems to infer emotions of natural persons in workplace and education institutions shall not be placed on market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: Emotion-recognition AI systems in workplace and education institutions
exceptions: Where intended for medical or safety reasons

rules.label: Prohibition of biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes
rules.obligation: Biometric categorisation systems that deduce or infer race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious/philosophical beliefs, sex life, or sexual orientation shall not be placed on market, put into service, or used.
rules.scope: Biometric categorisation AI systems
exceptions: Labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets
Categorisation of biometric data in the area of law enforcement

rules.label: Prohibition of real-time remote biometric ID in public spaces for law enforcement
rules.obligation: Use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement is prohibited unless strictly necessary for an exhaustively listed objective (targeted search for victims, prevention of substantial imminent threat/terrorist attack, or localisation of a suspect for serious offences in Annex II).
rules.scope: Law enforcement use of real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces
exceptions: Targeted search for specific victims of abduction, trafficking, sexual exploitation, or missing persons
Prevention of a specific, substantial and imminent threat to life/physical safety or a genuine present/foreseeable terrorist attack
Localisation/identification of a suspect for offences in Annex II punishable by at least four years custodial sentence

rules.label: Conditions for permitted real-time biometric ID use (FRIA and registration)
rules.obligation: Permitted use must confirm identity of a specifically targeted individual, account for the nature/consequences of the situation, comply with necessary and proportionate safeguards under national law, and only be authorised after completing a fundamental rights impact assessment (Article 27) and registering the system in the EU database (Article 49); in urgent cases registration may be completed without undue delay.
rules.scope: Law enforcement deployers of permitted real-time remote biometric identification
exceptions: In duly justified urgency, use may commence before EU database registration if completed without undue delay

rules.label: Prior judicial/administrative authorisation for real-time biometric ID use
rules.obligation: Each law enforcement use requires prior authorisation by a judicial or independent administrative authority upon reasoned request; in urgency, use may begin without authorisation provided it is requested within 24 hours, and if rejected use must stop immediately and all data/results discarded.
rules.scope: Law enforcement deployers of permitted real-time remote biometric identification
exceptions: Duly justified urgency: authorisation may be requested within 24 hours after commencement

rules.label: High-risk classification via Annex I safety component requiring third-party conformity assessment
rules.obligation: An AI system intended to be used as a safety component of a product (or which is itself a product) covered by Union harmonisation legislation in Annex I, and required to undergo third-party conformity assessment, shall be considered high-risk.
rules.scope: AI systems that are safety components or products under Annex I harmonisation legislation

rules.label: High-risk classification for Annex III systems
rules.obligation: AI systems referred to in Annex III shall be considered high-risk.
rules.scope: AI systems in Annex III use-case areas
exceptions: Art 6(3) derogation where no significant risk of harm

rules.label: Derogation: Annex III system not high-risk if it meets an exception and does not perform profiling
rules.obligation: An Annex III AI system shall not be considered high-risk where it does not pose a significant risk of harm and fulfils one of the listed conditions (narrow procedural task, improving a completed human activity, detecting deviations without replacing human assessment, or preparatory task), unless it performs profiling of natural persons.
rules.scope: Annex III AI systems meeting a derogation condition
exceptions: Always high-risk where the system performs profiling of natural persons

rules.label: Profiling always renders Annex III system high-risk
rules.obligation: Notwithstanding the derogation conditions, an Annex III AI system shall always be considered high-risk where it performs profiling of natural persons.
rules.scope: Annex III AI systems performing profiling of natural persons

rules.label: Provider must document non-high-risk assessment and register
rules.obligation: A provider who considers that an Annex III AI system is not high-risk shall document its assessment before placing on the market or putting into service, comply with the registration obligation in Article 49(2), and provide the documentation to national competent authorities upon request.
rules.scope: Providers claiming Annex III system is not high-risk under Art 6(3)

rules.label: AI system as safety component under Annex I harmonisation legislation
rules.obligation: An AI system intended to be used as a safety component of a product, or which is itself a product, covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I is subject to the high-risk classification and conformity requirements of this Regulation.
rules.scope: AI systems that are products or safety components of products covered by Annex I Union harmonisation legislation (machinery, toys, lifts, medical devices, motor vehicles, civil aviation, etc.)

rules.label: Remote biometric identification systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: Remote biometric identification systems permitted under Union or national law
exceptions: Biometric verification whose sole purpose is to confirm a person is who they claim to be

rules.label: Biometric categorisation by sensitive attributes is high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI systems for biometric categorisation according to sensitive or protected attributes

rules.label: Emotion recognition systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI systems for emotion recognition

rules.label: AI safety components in critical infrastructure are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI safety components in management/operation of critical digital infrastructure, road traffic, water, gas, heating, electricity

rules.label: Education and vocational training AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI for admission, evaluating learning outcomes, assessing education level, or monitoring prohibited behaviour during tests

rules.label: Employment and worker management AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI for recruitment, selection, task allocation, promotion/termination decisions, or performance monitoring

rules.label: Creditworthiness/credit scoring AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI for evaluating creditworthiness or establishing credit score of natural persons
exceptions: AI systems used solely for detecting financial fraud

rules.label: Essential public/private services AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI for eligibility for public benefits, insurance risk/pricing, or emergency call evaluation/dispatch

rules.label: Law enforcement AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI used by/for law enforcement for victim risk assessment, polygraphs, evidence reliability, offending risk, or profiling

rules.label: Migration, asylum and border control AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI for polygraphs, risk assessment of entrants, examination of asylum/visa applications, or person detection/identification

rules.label: Administration of justice and democratic processes AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: Classified as high-risk AI system subject to Chapter III requirements
rules.scope: AI assisting judicial authorities in fact/law interpretation, or influencing elections/referenda/voting behaviour
exceptions: AI tools used only for administrative/logistical campaign organisation where persons are not directly exposed to output

scenarios.label: HR screening tool
scenarios.description: AI ranks job applicants by predicted suitability for a role

scenarios.label: Creditworthiness assessment
scenarios.description: AI assesses creditworthiness for consumer loan applications

scenarios.label: Facial recognition (public space)
scenarios.description: Real-time biometric identification by law enforcement in a publicly accessible shopping centre

scenarios.label: Narrow procedural task (Art 6(3))
scenarios.description: HR system claiming Article 6(3) exception — performs a narrow preparatory task only, with human override

scenarios.label: Customer service chatbot
scenarios.description: General-purpose customer support assistant, no high-risk use case
